You want surface evidence to complement Tier 1 variance audit
You're doing source triage on a manuscript and want flagged sentences for adjudication

artifacts: compare JSON (per-signal Cliff's delta + Cohen's d, sorted by |delta|)

entries.purpose: aggregate the deterministic B4 stance + B3 abstraction signals plus the net-new AGD marker module (assuring/guarding/discounting) over a corpus into an argument-move population profile, and run the two-corpus separation test (Cliff's delta primary, Cohen's d secondary) — the cheap, judge-free half of ArgScope, gating any B1/B2 LLM-judge spend.
use_when: building an argument-register population baseline (the profiles the acquisition-tier registers feed)
answering whether deterministic signals separate two corpora (e.g. human vs LLM argument) before spending on the LLM judge

entries.purpose: Score how a public-debate essay's argument is BUILT against Kim et al. 2026's human / LLM anchors — the B1 structural arc (paragraph-role transition rates: support→proposal, support→support, thesis-opening) + the B2 discourse-mode mix (argumentation share). The argument-domain sibling of narrative_decision_audit; measures argumentative DIVERSITY, not quality or provenance.
use_when: Target is a public-debate / op-ed-register argumentative essay (≥ ~300 words, multi-paragraph)
You want structural collapse-tells that survive sentence-level rewriting
You want a descriptive pre-flag for whether a dialectical-clarity run is informative
do_not_use_when: You need an AI-vs-human provenance verdict or a quality judgment (the surface refuses both — diversity ≠ quality, no "human = better")
Target is research / legal / policy writing (the paper's anchors are register-bound to public-debate forums — the consumer's `distant` tier; structural signals only)
You don't have an LLM judge or a pre-computed label manifest

compute.cost_note: $0.03–0.20 per doc via Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini (per-paragraph labeling); free with --judge manifest

entries.purpose: Two-model perplexity comparison (Hans et al. 2024). Scores a target against a scorer LLM + observer LLM pair; lower ratios indicate the target is more predictable under the scorer than the observer — a known signal for AI-generated text. v1 uses perplexity-ratio; v2 uses true cross-perplexity when tokenizers align.
use_when: You want a discrimination-evidence signal that's close to the published 95% AUC condition
You have access to a scorer + observer pair (default tinyllama + gpt2)
Target ≥ 50 tokens
do_not_use_when: You need a binary verdict — ships uncalibrated by default
Target is in the scorer's or observer's training set (memorization biases the score)
You can't install the surprisal tier (transformers + torch, ~2 GB)

compute.cost_note: One-time ~600 MB tinyllama + ~500 MB gpt2 download; CPU inference acceptable

entries.purpose: A split-conformal abstention layer over any signal: given operator-supplied calibration nonconformity scores and a target score, emit a distribution-free, finite-sample conformal p-value and a prediction set at coverage 1-alpha. Turns "the band reads uncalibrated" into "abstain, with a guaranteed error rate at this operating point." A methodology wrapper, NOT a detector; an empty or full prediction set is a licensed abstention. The p-value is NOT P(AI).
use_when: You have calibration nonconformity scores for a reference class and want a guaranteed-coverage abstention on a target score
You want to convert an uncalibrated signal into a finite-sample, distribution-free decision
You want a two-class prediction set (supply --calibration-positive)

entries.purpose: Make the opaque authorship_embedding LUAR cosine (from voice_fingerprint) human-checkable by placing it side by side with SETEC's named interpretable stylometric features (burstiness, MATTR, MTLD, function-word ratio, dependency distance) and marking, per feature, whether the named lens TRACKS or DIVERGES from the embedding. An interpretation aid (Residualized Similarity arXiv:2510.05362 / Latent-Space Interpretation arXiv:2409.07072): the divergences are where the operator must inspect the neural signal on its own terms. Emits NO same-author / AI verdict (inherits authorship_embedding's refusals) and NO fabricated explained/residual number — you cannot partition a neural cosine without a fit, so a numeric split appears only under --fit-baseline (corpus-relative OLS). A diverging feature is an inspection pointer, never a suspicion score.
use_when: You have a LUAR cosine you want to make inspectable in named-feature terms
You want to see where interpretable stylometry tracks vs diverges from the embedding
You want to keep the human in the loop on an otherwise opaque similarity
do_not_use_when: You need a same-author / different-author / AI-vs-human verdict (the surface refuses all of these — it inherits authorship_embedding's refusals and adds none)
You want a single "how much is explained" number without a fit (v1 emits none; a corpus-relative split needs --fit-baseline)
You would read a diverging feature as a suspicion / authenticity / AI-ness score (it is an inspection pointer, not a score)

compute.cost_note: the named side-by-side is CPU/stdlib; the real-input path loads the LUAR style encoder via voice_fingerprint (~0.5–1.4 GB), so --inputs-json (precomputed) runs with no model

examples.description: Explain a precomputed cosine + features (no model load)
Real path (loads the LUAR encoder via voice_fingerprint)

do_not_use_when: You want an AI-provenance or authorship verdict (it produces a distance, not a call)

Target is under 500 words (the length floor)

examples.description: Spanish voice distance vs a same-language baseline

required: --lang language code shared by target and corpus (provenance)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: A language-agnostic, parser-free stylometric distance between a target and a baseline corpus — character n-grams, punctuation profile, token-/sentence- length distributions, and script statistics. No spaCy, no English assumption; runs on any Unicode script with zero model dependencies. Honest about its ceiling: language-agnostic, NOT language-aware (no morphology, no function-word list), so it refuses morphology-dependent voice claims and refuses cross-language comparison (the --lang tag is provenance).
use_when: You want a voice distance for non-English prose, or any language without an English parser
You have a register-matched, same-language baseline corpus and a target in the same language
Target is >= 500 words

do_not_use_when: You only need to know one specific dep (just run the script and read the error)
entries.purpose: Survey the SETEC framework's five dependency tiers (core, acquisition, ocr, calibration, surprisal) plus the optional power-ups tier. Reports what's installed, what's missing, and what the operator needs for the surface they're aiming at.
use_when: First-run after installing the plugin
An ImportError or "module not found" error fires from any SETEC script
Confirming readiness before running a calibration or replication batch

entries.purpose: Descriptive syntactic-SHAPE profile: the DISTRIBUTION of dependency distances (the linear span of each syntactic link, d = |i - head.i|) — the histogram, the adjacent-link share (d=1), and the long-range tail (d>=threshold). The scalar MDD (per-sentence mean/SD) already ships as variance_audit.mdd_stats and is REUSED here, not re-implemented; the distribution is the additive contribution (arXiv:2211.14620). Parser-tier (reuses the shared spaCy pipeline); abstains (available:false / missing_dependency) without it. NOT a verdict: not authorship/AI, not a quality/readability score, and NOT length-controlled (MDD covaries with sentence length; mean_sentence_length co-reported). Claim-license refuses those inferences; thresholds operator-side / PROVISIONAL.
use_when: You want a descriptive syntactic-complexity profile (how long-range a writer's dependencies run)
You want the dependency-distance distribution, not just the mean (which mdd_stats already gives)
Target is English, >= ~150 words, and a dependency parser is installed
do_not_use_when: No spaCy / en_core_web_sm available (the surface abstains)
You want an authorship/AI verdict or a writing-quality judgment
Non-English text (MDD norms are language-specific), or a length-controlled reading

compute.cost_note: spaCy dependency parse (CPU); reuses the shared variance_audit._NLP pipeline.

references: The distribution of syntactic dependency distances (arXiv:2211.14620): https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.14620

compute.cost_note: CPU + en_core_web_sm; regex extraction, light per-turn tagging.

The manuscript has little tagged dialogue (attribution is tag-heuristic, not coreference)

entries.purpose: Per-character dialogue-voice profiling + cross-character divergence (Round 2 of pov_voice_profile, which profiles narration). Extracts quoted spans and tag-attributes them to speakers ("said X" / "X asked"); unattributed dialogue is bucketed separately and never force-attributed. Per character: contraction rate, mean turn length + variance, dialogue-tag verb diversity, vocative rate, interruption / trailing-punctuation rate, top function words + discourse markers. Reports a descriptive cross-character divergence matrix + a list of the lowest-divergence pairs — NOT a verdict. Refuses author-identity, AI-provenance, and quality inference.
use_when: Checking whether a multi-character draft keeps its characters voice-distinct in dialogue
Character-voice collapse is suspected (it surfaces in dialogue before narration)
You want a per-character spoken-register profile to pair with pov_voice_profile's narration layer

entries.purpose: Clean-room EditLens-style document-level edit-magnitude estimate — a RoBERTa-family regressor trained with MSE against a BERTScore-style similarity proxy between pre/post-edit pairs, framed as a SAME-CORPUS calibrated estimate with explicit OOD caveats, never an absolute "% AI". Uncalibrated by default (score only, no band); a calibrated model is operator-supplied via --model and applies only to the corpus it was calibrated on. The claim-license refuses an absolute %-AI / dosage claim, cross-corpus generalization, and per-sentence localization, and states the OOD-collapse caveat (degree estimation works in-distribution, collapses toward binary out-of-distribution).
use_when: You have an operator-calibrated model for a SPECIFIC corpus and want a same-corpus edit-magnitude estimate
You're assembling a multi-signal evidence pack and want a document-level magnitude reading
You accept the in-distribution-only caveat

entries.purpose: Flag CANDIDATE rhetorical moves in an argument-shaped nonfiction passage against the Logic 13-type fallacy taxonomy (Jin et al. 2022, arXiv:2202.13758): per flag, a candidate_type + a verbatim span pointer + a Flee-the-Flaw implicit-logic reconstruction (arXiv:2406.12402) explaining why the judge flagged it. Descriptive priors for a human reviewer — NOT a verdict. Emits no soundness / quality / "bad argument" label, score, or aggregate; a flagged move may be legitimate in context. The argument-pattern sibling of argument_decision_audit (structural arc); this flags MOVES, not structure.
use_when: You want candidate fallacy-pattern flags with span pointers to triage for human review
Target is an argument-shaped nonfiction passage (op-ed / policy / testimony)
You want a Flee-the-Flaw reconstruction of each flagged move's implicit logic
do_not_use_when: You need a soundness / quality / "bad argument" / "fallacious" verdict (the surface refuses ALL of these — it flags CANDIDATE moves for a human, never rules; a flagged move is frequently legitimate in context)
You want an automated fallacy COUNT as a quality metric (candidate_pattern_tally is a rollup of flags raised for review, never a count of confirmed fallacies)
You don't have an LLM judge or a pre-computed label manifest

compute.cost_note: $0.01–0.10 per doc via Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini (one labeling call); free with --judge manifest or --judge mock (a deterministic test stub)

examples.description: Candidate-flag scan with a pre-computed judge manifest

entries.purpose: Fast-DetectGPT conditional-probability curvature (Bao et al. 2024). Scores a target under a single causal LM and reports a curvature z-score: the standardized gap between the text's conditional log-probability and the distribution of conditional log-probs for alternative tokens sampled from the model's own per-position conditional distributions. Higher curvature means the text sits nearer a local maximum of the model's probability surface — a zero-shot signal for machine generation. Orthogonal to Binoculars' cross-perplexity ratio and DivEye's surprisal moments.
use_when: You want a discrimination-evidence signal distinct from Binoculars (single model, curvature not ratio)
You're stacking orthogonal Surface-5 signals (Binoculars + curvature + DivEye)
Target ≥ 50 words and roughly in-distribution for the scoring model
do_not_use_when: You need a binary verdict — ships uncalibrated by default, no threshold
Target is out-of-distribution for the model, or may be in its training set (memorization biases the score)
The text has been paraphrased / surface-rewritten (curvature is paraphrase-sensitive)
You can't install the surprisal tier (transformers + torch)

compute.cost_note: one small causal LM, ~0.6–2 GB weights; CPU works, GPU faster

examples.description: T-Detect t-standardized SCORE curvature_t (the paper's statistic; robust to adversarial/paraphrased text; spec 25, arXiv:2507.23577); no p-value emitted; default gaussian unchanged

You're comparing formulaicity across documents or registers

entries.purpose: General Imposters attribution harness (Koppel et al. 2014, Kestemont et al. 2016 / R stylo::imposters()). Under bootstrap resampling with feature subsampling, measures how often a target falls closer to a candidate writer's identity baseline than to N plausible-other writers in matched register. Emits a win proportion (1.0 = always closer to candidate) with a bootstrap CI; scores near the extremes are trustworthy, the gray zone (~0.2-0.8) explicitly refuses an attribution claim. Licenses "stylometrically consistent/inconsistent with", never "is by". Privacy-gated: voiceprint-shaped outputs default under ai-prose-baselines-private/ and refuse public paths without --allow-public-output.
use_when: You want calibrated consistency evidence (target vs candidate-vs-impostor pool), not a raw distance — the embedding analogue is voice_fingerprint's n-way mode
You have a corpus manifest with the candidate's identity_baseline entries plus register-matched impostor personas
You can accept a refusal — the gray zone is a designed outcome, not an error
do_not_use_when: You need an authorship verdict in the legal or philosophical sense — the harness measures consistency with a stylometric fingerprint only
You have no register-matched impostor pool in the manifest (the MIN_IMPOSTORS gate refuses)
Impostor identities must stay anonymous in a public report and you cannot run the anonymization path

compute.cost_note: stdlib + scipy (CI computation); bootstrap iterations scale linearly

examples.description: GI run against a candidate persona with a manifest-backed impostor pool
examples.cmd: python3 plugins/setec-voiceprint/scripts/general_imposters.py --target draft.txt --manifest corpus_manifest.jsonl --candidate-persona blog --register blog_essay --json-out ai-prose-baselines-private/gi_report.json

The writer has a baseline corpus of ≥ 50K words
You want to detect post-revision mimicry / cosplay (paired with mimicry_cosplay_audit)
do_not_use_when: The baseline is mixed-genre (idiolect signals cross-contaminate across registers)
The baseline is under ~20K words (sparse n-grams produce false positives)

compute.cost_note: scipy for G² / PMI; no API costs

examples.description: Build a preservation list from a writer's baseline

compute.cost_note: embeddings; CPU-feasible but slow, GPU optional.

do_not_use_when: You want an AI/human verdict (this surface ships uncalibrated and refuses one)
You need values comparable across embedding models (PHD is embedding-model dependent)
Target is short (too few embedding units destabilizes the scaling fit)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: Clean-room persistent-homology dimension (PHD) of a text's contextual-embedding point cloud — a topological discrimination signal reported in the literature as the single most orthogonal axis to perplexity / lexical signals (Tulchinskii et al. 2023). Human prose tends to a higher intrinsic dimension than AI prose. Emits the scalar PHD plus sample size and the embedding-model id; uncalibrated (no band, no verdict) and the claim-license refuses an AI/human verdict absent operator-supplied thresholds.
use_when: You want a geometry-based discrimination axis orthogonal to perplexity / lexical signals
You're assembling a multi-signal evidence pack and want a topological reading
Target is >= 500 words (enough embedding units to fit the PHD scaling law)

entries.purpose: Cross-chapter aggregate of the Layer A distributional diagnostic. Runs variance_audit's signal machinery (audit_text / classify_compression) on every chapter of a manuscript (or every file in a chapter directory) and reports a dashboard of per-signal medians, per-chapter band labels, and outlier chapters that single-chapter audits miss.

entries.purpose: Detect lexical mimicry without syntactic conformity — the style-cosplay signature. Cross-checks idiolect-phrase survival (from an idiolect_detector JSON) against function-word / syntactic Delta (from a voice_distance JSON): normal authorial work shows the two negatively correlated; high survival WITH high Delta is the dissociation flag a composite score would hide. Also flags unnaturally high signature-phrase density (over-preservation). Refuses authorship verdicts: a cosplay flag is evidence the draft is not a normal authorial sample, not a call on whether it is AI, imitation, or self-conscious self-imitation.
use_when: A draft scores well on per-feature voice metrics but reads as imitation — you want the lexical-vs-syntactic cross-check, not an aggregate
QC on restoration / voice-matched output (catches over-targeted revisions that hit the metric but lost the voice)
You already have idiolect_detector and voice_distance JSON outputs for the same target (the cross-check needs both to fire shape 1)
do_not_use_when: You need an authorship or AI-provenance verdict — the surface refuses both
You have no idiolect / voice_distance JSONs for the target — the audit degrades to density-only evidence
You want a single composite mimicry score — the dissociation pattern is the signal; averaging the axes hides it

compute.cost_note: stdlib-only; reads pre-computed audit JSONs, no model loads

examples.description: Full cross-check with both upstream audits

use_when: Target is long-form fiction ≥ 2000 words
You want evidence that survives LAMP-style stylistic rewriting
You're stacking signals with Tier 1 / AIC / Binoculars

entries.purpose: Descriptive narratorial-distance / free-indirect-discourse (FID) profile for developmental editing of literary fiction: per-window pronoun anchoring, perception/cognition-verb density, deictic anchoring (proximal vs. distal), evaluative-adjective density, a heuristic FID score, an overall close/distant distribution, and a distance trajectory across document position. Adjacent to pov_voice_profile but NOT a voice-identity, authorship, AI, or quality instrument — measurements only (no band, no verdict); FID detection is heuristic, not a parse of literary intent.
use_when: You want to see where narration sits close to vs. distant from a character's consciousness, and how that moves across the text
You're developmentally editing literary fiction and want an FID / narratorial-distance trajectory
Target is long-form fiction >= 1500 words

entries.purpose: DJ-Search reconstructibility of a target against a reference pool (the operator's impostor pool by default) — the fraction of the target covered by long verbatim token spans (>= min_ngram, default 8) that appear anywhere in the reference corpus. Reports `coverage` (= reconstructibility) and `originality = 1 - coverage`, oriented gt = LESS reconstructible from that pool. A new set-level axis (target-vs-pool, not doc-vs-baseline). NOT an AI/human or plagiarism determination: clean-room DJ Search (Creativity Index, arXiv:2410.04265); measurements only, no band/verdict; the claim-license refuses provenance / plagiarism / quality inference.
use_when: You want to know how much of a target is reconstructible (recombined) from a reference pool you already hold (e.g. the impostor pool)
You're comparing reconstructibility across documents against a fixed pool
Target is >= ~24 words (>= min_ngram x 3)
do_not_use_when: You want an AI-provenance call (low originality is NOT 'AI'; this is not an AI signal)
You want a plagiarism / derivative-work / copyright determination (it is a measurement)
The reference pool is thin / register-mismatched (it inflates apparent originality)

inputs.reference: a --reference-dir of texts OR a --manifest (JSONL; id + text|text_path)

compute.cost_note: stdlib only; no models. Deterministic; substring index over the reference pool.

entries.purpose: Strictly baseline-relative productive-roughness deviation profile — fragment rate, sentence-initial coordinating-conjunction rate, contraction rate, adjacent-word-repetition rate, interjection/aside rate, very-short- sentence rate. Reports each as the draft's deviation (draft, baseline mean ± sd, z) from the WRITER'S OWN baseline, never an absolute roughness or quality judgment.

do_not_use_when: Manuscript is below ~2000 words — the distributional signals are noisy at small sizes (the audit warns; the warning is the answer)
You want a calibrated AI/human verdict — the band calls and signals are heuristic (regularization bands against priors), diagnostic only

examples.description: Punctuation cadence vs. a personal baseline

entries.purpose: Punctuation rhythm + interruption-grammar audit. Treats punctuation as its own diagnostic dimension: per-mark density, sentence-final distribution, interruption grammar (parentheticals / em-dashes / appositives), punctuation bigrams, and a compression-fraction band call. Punctuation regularizes before vocabulary erases, so this is among the earliest signals to fire on AI-edited or house-styled prose.
use_when: As a follow-up to Layer A when lexical compression fired and you want to name what specifically was regularized at the punctuation layer
A line edit / copyedit "changed something" and you want the specific cadence shifts (best with --baseline-dir for z-scores)
A quick triage on AI-revised prose — punctuation cadence shifts early

entries.purpose: Interpretable per-word stylometric divergence between a target and a baseline corpus — rank-turbulence divergence (RTD, in [0,1]) over the function-word distribution by default. Where voice_distance reports one aggregate Burrows Delta, RTD's per-word contributions say WHICH words drive the difference (top contributors over-ranked in the target vs the baseline). Additive companion to voice_distance (does NOT modify it). Clean-room RTD (Dodds et al. 2020; stylometric-Delta adaptation arXiv:2604.19499); stdlib, deterministic. NOT a verdict: a high RTD is not "different author" or "AI"; topic/genre/length shift it (especially in --all-words mode). Claim-license refuses any authorship/AI inference; thresholds operator-side / PROVISIONAL.
use_when: You want an interpretable, per-word complement to a voice_distance Burrows Delta run
You want to see which (function) words drive a target's divergence from a baseline
Target and baseline are >= ~200 words and register-matched
do_not_use_when: You want an authorship or AI-provenance verdict (this is a divergence measurement)
Target and baseline are topic-mismatched (the divergence becomes topical, not stylistic; --all-words mode is explicitly topical)
Either text is under ~200 words (unstable rank distribution)

inputs.baseline: a --baseline-dir of texts OR a --manifest (JSONL; id + text|text_path)

compute.cost_note: stdlib only; no models. Deterministic; counting + ranking + arithmetic.

entries.purpose: Raidar-style rewriting-invariance discrimination evidence: prompt an LLM to "rewrite to improve clarity," then measure how much it changed the text (edit-distance / token-overlap) averaged over N trials. Low change ⇒ more AI-like, high ⇒ more human-like. A paraphrase-robust axis orthogonal to perplexity. Uncalibrated: emits the distance + provenance only (no band, no verdict); strongly dependent on the judge model + exact prompt (both pinned in provenance). Clean-room implementation of the published idea (Mao et al., ICLR 2024) — no Raidar repo code/data vendored.
use_when: You want a discrimination signal that survives paraphrase attacks on perplexity detectors
You have an operator-supplied LLM (API key or local instruct model) to do the rewriting
You're stacking signals and want an axis orthogonal to surprisal / Binoculars

entries.purpose: Measures the agreement between the framework's surfaced candidates and an operator's triage judgments on a labeled item set: confusion matrix, percent agreement, Cohen's kappa, the prevalence-and-bias-adjusted kappa (PABAK), and a seeded bootstrap CI on kappa. Closes the loop the framework keeps open on principle ("source triage is judgment work"). Measures CONCORDANCE, not correctness — it never decides who is right.

You want to know whether the framework's candidate-surfacing matches expert reads on a register
You have at least 10 triaged items

entries.purpose: Empirical validation harness for SETEC task-surface outputs. Computes FPR / TPR / AUC / AUPRC with Wilson / Clopper-Pearson CIs against a labeled manifest, enforces prompt-level grouping, and refuses to mix scores across task surfaces because the surfaces answer different questions.

This is the within-author reference (the aggregation voice_distance / idiolect_detector / mimicry_cosplay_audit build on), not a diagnostic verdict.

entries.purpose: Report Toulmin critical-question COVERAGE for an argument-shaped nonfiction passage: for each major claim a pluggable LLM judge identifies, whether the warrant (inferential link), the backing for that warrant, and a rebuttal (counterargument / exception) are present, partial, or absent (Favero et al. 2024, "Critical Questions of Thought", arXiv:2412.15177). Descriptive coverage for a human reviewer — what questions the text answers vs leaves open. The warrant sibling of fallacy_scan under the same argument_pattern_scan surface; emits no soundness / quality / "unsound" label, score, or aggregate. An absent warrant/backing/rebuttal is a coverage GAP to examine, not a flaw.
use_when: You want per-claim warrant / backing / rebuttal coverage to triage for human review
Target is an argument-shaped nonfiction passage (op-ed / policy / testimony)
You want to see which Toulmin critical questions the text leaves open
do_not_use_when: You need a soundness / quality / "weak argument" / "unsound" verdict (the surface refuses ALL of these — coverage is descriptive; an absent warrant is a gap to examine, not a flaw, since many strong arguments leave a warrant implicit)
You want a coverage SCORE as a quality metric (coverage_summary is a descriptive per-question count, never a score)
You don't have an LLM judge or a pre-computed label manifest

compute.cost_note: $0.01–0.10 per doc via Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini (one labeling call); free with --judge manifest or --judge mock (a deterministic test stub)

examples.description: Warrant-coverage probe with a pre-computed judge manifest

entries.purpose: Green-list z-test (the KGW *construction* of Kirchenbauer et al. 2024, arXiv:2301.10226) — the first watermarking axis in SETEC. Scores a target against THIS module's Voiceprint-defined green-list partition (partition_prf `voiceprint-greenlist-v1/sha256-seed+pyrandom-shuffle`: a SHA-256(key, context) seed feeding a deterministic shuffle), NOT the official KGW reference processor's seeding schemes (simple_1 / selfhash / minhash) or its RNG device. Given a target + the OPERATOR-SUPPLIED parameters (key, gamma, hash_scheme, vocab), computes the green-list z-statistic + p-value (repeated n-grams scored once) and reports them as one per-signal card with a PROVISIONAL detection-power band. Model-free: a hash-seeded vocabulary partition + a green-token count + a one-proportion z-score. A positive is watermark-consistent with THIS partition, never "AI"; a low/zero z (or no key) is `unknown`, never "human" — absence is NOT evidence of human authorship. Keyed, parameterized, never sniffed; no is_watermarked / is_ai / is_human / verdict field.
use_when: You generated the tokens with THIS module''s green-list partition (partition_prf `voiceprint-greenlist-v1/sha256-seed+pyrandom-shuffle`) under a known key / gamma / hash_scheme / vocab and want positive, model-free evidence the stream carries it
You want that evidence for ONE named partition to sit beside the stylometric signals in an evidence pack
You can supply a token-id stream + vocab matching THIS partition''s tokenizer/vocab
do_not_use_when: The tokens were produced by the OFFICIAL KGW reference processor (simple_1 / selfhash / minhash) or any vendor / third-party watermark generator — this custom SHA/Python partition is not byte-compatible with their PRF/RNG and will systematically FALSE-NEGATIVE; you need a generator fingerprint matching partition_prf first
You have no key — the probe is keyed and cannot guess the scheme (scanning for unknown watermarks is the watermark-stealing attack, out of scope)
You want an AI / human / is_watermarked verdict — the surface ships no threshold and no verdict field, and the claim license forbids thresholding z / p_value / band into one
You would read a negative as human — absence is NOT evidence of human authorship (blind to other schemes, semantic / SynthID-class watermarks, and scrubbed watermarks)
The text may be paraphrase-scrubbed (detection power decays under heavy rewrite)
The scheme is semantic / SynthID / Gumbel-class (a different family with a different detector — v1 is token-level KGW green-list only)

inputs.key: the green-list hash seed (operator-supplied; never echoed)
inputs.vocab: {token: id} JSON map or an int vocab size
inputs.tokens: optional operator token-id stream (the real detection path)

compute.cost_note: stdlib counting + arithmetic; no model, no GPU, no network

examples.description: Real path — operator token-id stream + vocab matching the watermarking tokenizer
Labelled whitespace fallback (toy/demo/CI only — under-detects real BPE watermarks)
